prompt:
You are Agent 9 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-FndBIu.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook).

Specifically:
1. Check coordination context — the previous session (Session 8) added SMS support and flagged that user preferences need updating.
2. Review the current user preferences model and service from Session 3.
3. The original preferences assumed email-only. This assumption is no longer valid — SMS was added in Session 8.
4. Refactor the preferences model to support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook).
5. Update the preferences service to handle multi-channel preferences.
6. Update the notification service to check per-channel preferences before sending.
7. Update tests to cover multi-channel scenarios.
8. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- This is a conscious decision change — the email-only assumption from Session 3 is explicitly invalidated.
- Preserve backward compatibility where possible (existing email preferences should still work).
- Document the decision change and why.

parameters.task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook). Refactor from email-only to per-channel settings.

parameters.summary: Refactor notification preferences from email-only to per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook) using shared ChannelNotificationPreferences interface
parameters.context: Session 8 added SMS adapter support but the preferences model still assumed email-only. Users could not opt out of SMS or webhook notifications — a UX and compliance issue. This supersedes the Session 3 decision that assumed email was the only channel.
parameters.rationale: All three channels (email, SMS, webhook) share the same preference shape: enabled flag, frequency, and per-category opt-in/out. Using a single ChannelNotificationPreferences interface avoids code duplication while maintaining per-channel independence. Backward compatibility preserved via type aliases (EmailNotificationPreferences = ChannelNotificationPreferences, etc.) and repository fallback defaults for records that predate multi-channel support. The preferences check in NotificationService.send() already passes the channel to isNotificationEnabled(), so no changes needed there — only PreferencesService needed updating.

alternatives.option: Channel-specific interfaces with different fields (e.g. SMS has maxLength, webhook has retryPolicy)
alternatives.reason_rejected: All three channels need the same core preferences (enabled, frequency, categories). Channel-specific delivery config already lives in adapter config, not user preferences. Over-engineering for current requirements.

alternatives.option: Keep email-only preferences, add separate SmsPreferences and WebhookPreferences models
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would triple the code with identical structures. The unified ChannelNotificationPreferences eliminates duplication while type aliases maintain backward compatibility.

assumptions: All channels share the same category structure (orderConfirmations, orderStatusUpdates, welcomeMessages, accountUpdates)
New users default to all channels enabled — opt-out model
Channel-specific delivery configuration (e.g. SMS max length, webhook retry) belongs in adapter config, not user preferences

parameters.reason: Resolved: preferences model now supports email, SMS, and webhook channels with per-channel settings

parameters.summary: Agent 9: Multi-channel notification preferences implemented — email/SMS/webhook per-channel settings, 35 preference tests, all 203 tests pass
parameters.detail:
## What was done

### Modified files
- `src/models/user-preferences.ts` — Added `ChannelNotificationPreferences`, `CategoryPreferences`, `NotificationFrequency` as generic types. Added `sms` and `webhook` fields to `UserNotificationPreferences`. Kept backward-compat aliases (`EmailNotificationPreferences`, `EmailCategoryPreferences`, `EmailFrequency`). Updated `createDefaultPreferences()` to include all three channels.
- `src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts` — Updated `toDomain()`/`toRecord()` to serialize/deserialize SMS and webhook preferences. Added fallback defaults for records that predate multi-channel support.
- `src/services/preferences.service.ts` — Added `updateSmsPreferences()` and `updateWebhookPreferences()` methods. Refactored `isNotificationEnabled()` to check per-channel preferences for email, SMS, and webhook (in-app still defaults to allowed). Extracted shared `updateChannelPreferences()` private method.
- `src/index.ts` — Exports new types: `ChannelNotificationPreferences`, `CategoryPreferences`, `NotificationFrequency`, `SmsNotificationPreferences`, `WebhookNotificationPreferences`.
- `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` — Added 20 new tests covering: SMS/webhook default preferences, SMS/webhook update methods, per-channel preference checking, cross-channel independence, backward compatibility.

### Design
- All channels share `ChannelNotificationPreferences` (enabled + frequency + categories)
- Backward compatible: deprecated type aliases, repository fallback defaults
- `NotificationService.send()` unchanged — already delegated to `PreferencesService.isNotificationEnabled()` which now handles all channels
- Supersedes decision 01KN740BARXY2F7A049F2RMSSK (email-only assumption)

### Results
- TypeScript compiles cleanly
- All 203 tests pass (35 preferences tests, up from 15)
